source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A nucleus at rest undergoes $\alpha$-decay according to the equation:$_{92}^{225}X \rightarrow Y + \alpha$At time $t=0$, the emitted $\alpha$-particle enters in a region of space where a uniform magnetic field $\overrightarrow{B} = B_0\hat{i}$ and electric field $\overrightarrow{E} = E_0\hat{i}$ exist. The $\alpha$-particle enters in the region with velocity $\overrightarrow{v} = v_0\hat{j}$ from $x=0$. At time $t = \sqrt{3} \times 10^7 \frac{m_\alpha}{q_\alpha E_0}$ sec, the particle was observed to have speed twice the initial velocity $v_0$. The initial velocity $v_0$ of the $\alpha$-particle $.
